description_long: This object is a pair of pliers featuring robust, straight handles coated in a bright red, likely plastic or rubberized insulation for improved grip and electrical safety. The handles are slightly contoured and end in a flared guard near the pivot to prevent slippage during use. The jaws are made of dark, metallic material and are flat with a broad, blunt tip, suitable for gripping, bending, or cutting wires and other materials. The pivot mechanism is a single, fixed joint, allowing the jaws to open and close smoothly. The overall construction suggests durability and utility for various urban repair or maintenance tasks. No visible branding or logo is present on the tool.
description: A sturdy pair of red-handled pliers with flat, blunt metallic jaws and a fixed pivot joint, designed for gripping or cutting tasks. The handles are insulated and contoured for comfort and safety.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the red insulated handles and the flat, blunt metallic jaws.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the open jaws and the inner gripping surfaces, with both handles visible from the end.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the handles and the metallic jaws from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the handles and the closed pivot area, with the jaws less visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, insulated, contoured plastic handles with flared guard for slip prevention.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat, blunt jaws with broad gripping surfaces, suitable for general gripping and cutting; no serrations or needle-nose features.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Single fixed pivot joint, non-adjustable, standard for basic pliers.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth plastic with subtle contouring for ergonomic grip; no visible ribbing or knurling.
